entry.title: Re: Object Development for Side by side Extension
entry.content: The "E" of RICEFW is for enhancement/extension, so you can use this acronym. But even RICEFW in the old time could be not precise enough.

entry.title: Re: Streamline Data Migration from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: Selective Data Transition with Time-Slice
entry.content: @mahendra I checked and updated the blog post again - on my side it looks good now - thanks for being my patient tester 🙂

entry.title: Re: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!
entry.content:
hello @Yumnahabbasi

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2502 Release – What’s New?
entry.content:
This blog will give you an overview of our recent release in SAP Business Network for Logistics. It is intended for shippers and solution owners, carriers, and data providers as well as sales and presales colleagues who already have a detailed understanding of SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

The 2502 release brough three exciting features for shippers and carriers related to the settlement process in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration:

  • New invoice processing settings for seamless submission after dispute
  • Share SAP Transportation Management invoice status
  • Add VAT ID or Tax ID as document reference

However, let’s start with a cross-solution topic.

 

Cross SAP Business Network for Logistics

The Project44 unified API now supports road FTL tracking. For details see Configuration and Application Guide for project44 Add-Ons.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Performance monitoring through integration with SAP Cloud ALM

Performance monitoring helps IT administrators, identify and resolve critical issues related to end user performance and provides transparency in the actual performance experienced by end users.

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration is now integrated with SAP Cloud ALM to support centralized performance monitoring. You can use the ‘Real User Monitoring’ app provided by SAP Cloud ALM to measure all real user requests from dispute, tracking, and dock appointment applications.

For more information, please refer to:

  • Setup and Administration Guide
  • Real User Monitoring

Contracting: View aggregated dangerous goods information

Carriers can now view aggregated dangerous goods information in the detail pages even when the ordering party has used business add-in (BAdI) in their external system. Dangerous goods information is available in the following apps: ‘Freight Requests for Quotation’, ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’, ‘Confirm Freight Bookings (Air)’, ‘Confirm Freight Bookings (Ocean)’.

The aggregation has led to the following enhancements:

  •  Duplicate dangerous goods information (e.g. on different component levels) only show once
  •  Dangerous goods information is aggregated e.g. for a container that contains different packages and products out of which some are dangerous goods

 

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Show status and reschedule grouped appointments

In the Manage Dock Appointments and Self-Book Dock Appointments apps, shippers and carriers can now see the status of a grouped appointment and reschedule the same.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Display reference documents by priority

If there are several reference documents available for an appointment in the ‘Self-Book Dock Appointments’ app, the reference documents are now displayed following a priority sequence. If available, freight order is prioritized over yard order over transportation unit appointment.

To view all reference document types, open the appointment details and navigate to the ‘Reference Document’ tab.

 Dock Appointment Scheduling: API supports display of preferred slots

The ‘Provider API for Dock Appointment Scheduling’ now supports the display of preferred slots in the ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Self-Book Dock Appointments’ apps. Before, preferred slots were only supported with the TransportationOrderRequest message.

For more information see Provider API for Dock Appointment Scheduling.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Slot was renamed from ‘Preferred’ to ‘Acceptable’

In the ‘Self-Book Dock Appointments’ app the slots preferred by shipper, were renamed from ‘Preferred’ to ‘Acceptable’.

Settlement: New invoice processing settings for seamless submission after dispute

With this new feature we help ensure carriers get paid faster, as carriers can decide how they want to handle invoices after dispute resolution.

 Carriers can now select how to process the invoice after dispute resolution. There are three options:

  • Submit directly to your ordering party.
  • Navigate to the invoice e.g. to add an attachment and submit then.
  • Reject the invoice and create an entirely new one with changes in charges or charge types.

Upon first submission of a new dispute in the “Manage Disputes” application after the 2502 feature release, the carrier will get a message in the header section explaining the status of the invoice after dispute resolution.

There is also a link to change the “Invoice Process Settings”. There will be three options:

  • “Submitted to your ordering party”

The agreed charges will be updated in the invoice according to the amounts accepted in the dispute and it will automatically be submitted to your shipper.

  • “Ready for submission”

The agreed charges will be updated and the invoice will be set to status ‘ready for submission’. The invoice still needs to be submitted to the shipper. Choose this option when you want to update taxes or cancel the invoice.

  • “Rejected”

Initial invoice is set to status ‘Rejected’. Go back to the freight document to create a new invoice. Choose this option when you need to change charge codes or add charges.

Please note that the default processing of invoices created via API is different. Upon dispute resolution, these invoices go to status ‘rejected’. Carriers still have the option to select one of the three ‘Invoice Processing Settings’, though.

Following the above changes, the following new message types are now available for the settlement disputes scenario:

  • TransportationDisputeCaseNotification_Out (Dispute Case Notification to Shipper System)
  • TransportationDisputeCaseNotification_In (Dispute Case Notification from Shipper System)

Settlement: Share SAP Transportation Management invoice status

This feature will allow better visibility of the entire end to end invoice integration. The carriers will be able to know when the invoice got created in the shipper backend system, when it is being processed or when the invoice gets rejected.

The feature can be utilized by shippers by means of implementing a new SOAP message:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3557909/E

 

 

 

Settlement: Add VAT ID or Tax ID as document references

VATID and TAXID have been added as new document types, thus enabling carriers to share their VAT/tax ID via the document reference functionality.

To enable this feature, shippers need to enable the functionality to send reference documents.

 

  

SAP Innovation Awards 2025

The submission deadline for customers and partners to participate in the 2025 Innovation Awards is approaching quickly! Please hand in your pitch decks before March 14th, 2025.

 

 

Pease share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2502. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2502 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Need to understand how sales based rentals work from lessee perspective.
entry.content:
In my project, my client (lessee) needs to pay rentals based on sales (for example: basic rent or 10% of turnover rent, whichever is higher). Vendor calculates sales on yearly basis and then compares the turnover rent (10% of turnover) with basic rent. The difference (if any) will be invoiced by vendor at year end. 
My client wants to do expense accrual on a monthly basis and not wait for the invoice from vendor. In order to do that, my client compares the cumulative actual sales with cumulative break even sales to arrive at the expense accrual amount on a monthly basis. Could you please suggest how we can build this rule on SAP REFX

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery incorrectly received and putaway in...
entry.content: Thanks Ajit, PU Withdrawn qty exceeded error in SMQ2 is due to the fact that Inbound delivery B was processed against Outbound delivery A incorrectly. Please note item and quantity in Inbound delivery A are different to Item and quantity in Outbound delivery A. So I dont know how to move forward. This mistake shouldnt have happened maybe we need more validations there to ensure the correct deliveries match? Im not sure how tolerance settings can help here. Please expand. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Packaging material information not flowing from Shipm...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

You can use include "ZXV54U03" for determine packing material in cost calculation. The code is below.

 

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <wa_vbplk> TYPE vbplk.

READ TABLE i_refobj-vbplk INDEX 1 ASSIGNING <wa_vbplk>.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
  c_komp-vhilm = <wa_vbplk>-vhilm.
ELSE.
  CLEAR c_komp-vhilm.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Admin user cannot be created - Email already exists
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Currently one our customer is facing the issues with Registering for CDC console, user encountering as User Already Exists. But user is not there in Administrators section

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Admin user cannot be created - Email already exists
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

If an administrator was deleted from the console, his/her profile still exists in our internal database with the email address.

On the provided screenshot you can see "Have an account already?" line and below that there is a "Click Here" button.

User needs to click there and login with credentials, then the admin profile will be created.

You can refer SAP Note 0003449643 - Admin user can't be created.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

Best regards,

Eberechukwu Nwaobiora

entry.title: Re: switching source systems from DEV to QA for BW dev

This is a hard one, so I'm just guessing here and trying to give tips to fix it by elimination.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: I cannot post a VL01N material document because a complete picking was carried out, but the material
entry.content: I cannot post a VL01N material document because a complete picking was carried out, but the material to be issued will be less, so the packaging is partial. How do I be able to account for the material document or delivery note for the smaller amount that will actually come out? with WM in SAP

entry.title: SAP Advanced SQL Migration version 3.5.4 available as of February 2025
entry.content:
SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool version 3.5.4 is now available with the latest updates, enhancements and fixes. Documentation, blog updates and deep details about this tool are shared in the official SAP community:

          SAP Advanced SQL Migration

The new 3.5.4 release is available for download through the SAP Hana tools download page:

          SAP Hana tools download page

SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool v.3.5.4 provides the following enhancements and fixes:

  1. Gateway support for SAP Java virtual machine (SapMachine/SAPVM).
  2. Latest SAP databases certified:
          · HANA Cloud QRC4-24.
          · HANA SP08.
          · ASE 16 SP4 PL6.
          · IQ 16.1 SP05 PL14.
          · SQL Anywhere 17.0 SP1 PL18.
  3. Oracle DBMS_LOCK and SQL Server application locks partial conversion using native application locks for SAP HANA 2.0 SP08 and HANA CLOUD.
  4. Specific SQL Server fixes/improvements:
          · Bug extracting geography columns in MSSQL.
          · HASHBYTES conversion implemented for algorithms MD5 and SHA2_256.
          · SQL Server update with top clause conversion improvements.
          · Gateway encrypted connections for SQL Server.
  5. Issues extracting and converting synonyms in some situations (Oracle and SQL Server) are now fixed and improvements implemented.
  6. Problem with "extract" and "extact_offline" options hanging in some situations is fixed.
  7. New parameter #706 added for SAP HANA and SAP HANA CLOUD targets to get separate create table/view scripts for each table/view.
  8. Gateway Free Open-Source components updated to latest version.
  9. Bug fixes for different source-target database combinations.
  10. General conversion improvements.

Enjoy SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool version 3.5.4.

 

Contact Advanced_SQL_Migration@sap.com if you need further help or have further questions/clarifications.

 

Community: SAP Advanced SQL Migration 

Contact:  Advanced_SQL_Migration@sap.com

entry.title: Re: SAP User Roles and SAP Ariba User Groups
entry.content:
Ariba groups are different from SAP groups.

The way it usually works in a project is that you extend existing SAP roles with Ariba groups/permissions (storing Ariba in Z tables in SAP).

then these extended SAP roles are created as custom/new groups in Ariba which is what you said really:
"I also understand that before extracting user master data from the SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA on-premise, users must manually maintain all the SAP Roles as User Groups in the SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing solution. If this does not happen, the upload of user master data will fail. I believe that means it is important to ensure any SAP User Roles are also SAP Ariba User Groups."

lastly you import users and associated groups into Ariba.. the mapping you did in step above will allow all users to receive proper Ariba groups based on what you imported

entry.title: Re: RE-FX Leasing valuation Rule Poland and Czech GAAP in...
entry.content:
Hi Shrinivas,

While 1T6 test script is not directly customized for Poland, you can also check out scope item 21P, which covers the same general topic you are looking for. The SSCUIs you are using are available and maintained for all countries (except TR).

When creating new entries in Condition Valuation group SSCUI 103405, new entries will always start with the letter Z - that's a system design. You can assign these Condition Valuation Groups to your required Valuation Rules in SSCUI 102117 as you are showing in your screenshot (which is also where you can assign the Accounting Principle used).

Best Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Object Development for Side by side Extension
entry.content: Wrong tag: question not related to "SAP Business One Extensibility", which is related to "SAP Business One". Question is about S/4. "SAP Business One" is its own SAP product, which has nothing to do with S/4 or ABAP.

Nuno Raposo

entry.title: confidential enterprise projects
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My client runs ETO processes in their business and regularly contract with military organizations. This generates additional S&A requirements as in some cases, the project information along with any associated transaction data needs to be restricted to users that are actively working on the project or on a need to know basis. My first thought is to use a cost center, but when digging, I noticed that enterprise projects have an authorization group and a confidential project field in I_EnterpriseProject. Is there any info on how to access/utilize the fields to restrict the visibility of the data.

Thanks

Tom

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Supplier Evaluation Steps
entry.content:
Dear @Ruthvik_Chowdary,

thank you very much for a detailed explanation how to deal with supplier evaluation. 
Unfortunatelly I'm struggling to get the notification once the Start Group-based evaluation is submitted. I checked all the settings (incl. email and communication language) and I'm sending the evaluation request to myself so I'm using the CB user with set e-mail. Hence no request to evaluate received. Could you please advise what could be the root cause?

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: User Type for SAP CPI OData Adapter Usage
entry.content:
We are working on a CPI iFlow where we are calling an OData service which is residing in our on-prem system. Our on-prem system will be called via an APP ID(not dialog user). We created a security key for that in CPI tenant. On the adapter configuration, we are using basic auth and sending the security key. For OData configuration, when we go for connection to backend, on the popup screen it fails to fetch the metadata with error message "Please enter valid credentials". 

We later changed the type of the user(which we used in the security material) from "system" to "service" and it worked. Is there any mandate for this or can it be worked with other type of users as well? 

 

SAP Integration Suite SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway @PriyankaChak @priyankachaksap

entry.title: Deep dive into the Fit-to-Standard Workshops to streamline SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud Implementation
entry.content:
Fit-to-Standard Workshops have emerged as a pivotal element in streamlining SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud implementations. These workshops provide a structured approach to aligning business processes with SAP's best practices, ensuring a smoother and more efficient transition. In this deep dive, we will explore the intricacies of Fit-to-Standard Workshops, shedding light on how they can significantly enhance your SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud journey.

Level 1: Fit-to-Standard (High-Level Overview)

  • Purpose: To provide an initial high-level review of SAP's standard processes and functionality.
  • Details: This is typically a broad and general overview where you will identify the key business areas (e.g., finance, procurement, sales) and examine how SAP’s standard features map to those areas.
  • Content: The team would explain the major processes in SAP (like order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, etc.) and how they align with the organization's requirements at a very high level.
  • Outcome: It is expected that the team will identify key gaps or customization needs, but the focus is more on alignment and understanding if the business processes can be supported without deep configuration changes.
  • Detail Level: Minimal, with an emphasis on alignment and understanding. At this stage, you would generally not be making decisions yet.

Level 2: Fit-to-Standard (Detailed Process Walkthrough)

  • Purpose: To dive deeper into specific business processes and how they will be executed within the SAP system.
  • Details: This session gets into more detail, where functional experts from both the business side and SAP consultants assess specific business processes in SAP. They go through each process step-by-step to identify potential gaps or needed enhancements.
  • Content: Each business process (such as order entry, invoice processing, or inventory management) will be reviewed in more depth, with a focus on how SAP’s standard functionality meets those requirements. Customization and configuration needs are explored more deeply.
  • Outcome: Gaps are identified, but solutions are also discussed—whether a configuration change can resolve the gap or if a custom development is needed. The goal is to finalize the understanding of the process and the level of adjustment required.
  • Detail Level: High, focusing on specific functionalities and features in SAP and how they will work for the organization.

Level 3: Fit-to-Standard (Very Detailed, Solution-Oriented)

  • Purpose: To finalize the detailed mapping between SAP's functionality and the business requirements and finalize decisions on any gaps or customizations.
  • Details: This level focuses on very granular details, down to configuration settings, business rules, and potential areas for custom developments. This stage ensures that the configuration of the SAP system is aligned precisely with business needs and that all gaps are thoroughly identified.
  • Content: Detailed process flows will be reviewed, often using case studies, test scenarios, and prototypes to validate whether SAP can fulfill the requirements as is, or if there will be a need for complex configurations or custom development. Integration points with other systems may also be reviewed.
  • Outcome: The final configuration decisions are made, and a clear plan is put in place for any customization or adjustments needed. Key deliverables might include detailed configuration specifications and documentation.
  • Detail Level: Very high, focused on resolving every single gap, configuration issue, or customization that could arise in the implementation.

In summary:

  • Level 1: High-level, broad review of business processes and alignment with SAP standard.
  • Level 2: Detailed examination of key business processes to identify gaps or customization needs.
  • Level 3: Highly detailed, solution-oriented discussions to finalize configurations and customizations

#SAP #S4Hana #CloudImplementation #FitToStandard #DigitalTransformation #BusinessProcess #ERP #CloudSolutions #SAPWorkshops

entry.title: Tax difference for supplier invoices in S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client is facing an external tax amount rounding difference/variance issue as compared to the actual tax rates which was noticed recently but has been occurring since project implementation nearly 3 years ago.

The accounts payable sales tax calculated in our S/4 HANA cloud production system for few supplier invoices don't match with the different tax rates (based on the region) confirmed by our external tax partner viz. Thomson Reuters Onesource. Below are the various tax rates confirmed by Onesource for your reference:

Also below is a screenshot of few supplier invoices and the tax that got applied in these cases (tax rates of items that have the differences are highlighted in red):

Kindly advise what could be done to fix this issue.

Regards,

Alan

entry.title: Re: How to perform the Unit Test where the product code i...
entry.content: There are different approaches to address this issue. You can use DAO's to mock the database access. You can also use the SQL Testdouble framework.

entry.title: Re: Handling Overflow of Characters in <Ustrd> Tag During...
entry.content:
Hi nejla,

 

so in general what happens in an overflow case, when external files are imported:

The rest is cut off and "lost".

If the data element can only hold 140 characters, the other stuff would not be stored anywhere.

 

In general in such a case, my recommendation would be to use payment advices and enter the information of those invoices etc. there. I guess that is the reason why you want to have all information also visible there, because you most likely have there more than just 1-2 invoice reference numbers which are mentioned.

 

For such cases we have the payment advice process. Because the payment advices can contain even more information and would make it possible for you to clear with one payment transaction hundreds of invoices.

 

Other than that I have currently nothing else which would help you. But I would like to ask some questions back:

Does this happen often in your business? What release are you on?

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Sap Customer checkout Monitor Inconsistent status for some articles posting entries
entry.content:
Dear Experts ,

Recently an inquiry came from a one of our customers whose having cafe's and Minimarts . So one of there minimart has two pos machines and when do sales from those checkouts most of the transactions are stuck in the sap customer checkout monitor not posting to the SAP business one Hana backend and when checked those inconsistent entries , error message displays as below ,

004 - SQL Error (
0010000108):
SAP DBTech JDBC: [339]: invalid number: Failed to parse constant EA

 Sap customer checkout V2.0 FP18 PL16

Sap business One V10 Hana FP2111

So if anyone of you can give us an advice to overcome this matter , It's greatly appreciated .

Thanks on advance .

Best Regards,

Tharindu

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Cloud Public - Standard Positive Pay output...
entry.content:
Hello, thank you for your question.

You maintain the these attributes for positive files in format mapping properties. You can refer to the step 1 and step 2 in this documentation Business Process for Payment Medium Formats | SAP Help Portal to create a payment medium format, and maintain the format mapping properties.

 This documentation on for the Create Positive Pay Files app is also for your reference: Create Positive Pay Files | SAP Help Portal

Kind regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RF - List of Exceptiones Codes as a Pushbutton

entry.title: Manual Bank Reconciliation using manage bank statements
entry.content:
In manage Bank statement, I will do BRS manually, Where i want to post in 1 step posting(Posting Area 1) and it needs to get cleared manually, instead of manual clearing.

For outgoing bank reconciliation, the journal entry needs to be like this

Outgoing bank Dr

Main Bank Cr

For Incoming bank reconciliation, the journal entry needs to be like this

Incoming Bank CR

Main Bank Dr

it can't be done using standard posting rule, So i need to create new processing rule using the tile "manage processing rule" How can i do it. Kindly suggest me a solution.

entry.title: Is the cross selling functionality available in the public cloud?
Re: CDS Collumn concatenate?
entry.content: Hi, sorry for the late reply thank you very much for your answer, I implemented this solution and it works perfectly.

entry.title: Re: CDS Collumn concatenate?
entry.content: Hi Thank you very much for your reply, yes that was what I was looking for.

entry.title: Depreciation Key configuration with Fiscal year differentiation
entry.content:
Hello dear community,

the client is requiring us to create a new depreciation key for a single chart of depreciation which is assigned to a CoCd.

The requirement is the following:

- Depreciation is calculated linear and ordinary based on the useful life of the asset

- In the first FY of the asset activation depreciation is calculated and posted by 50% of the basis depreciation for this period

- Depreciation starts in the next month after acquisition

- From the FY+1 after activation onwards depreciation is calculated and posted normally (100%)

 

Example:

Acquisition = 3600 €; Useful life = 3 years (36 months); Acquisition/Capitalization date = 01.04.2024; CoCd FY start = 01.01.YYYY; monthly calculated depreciation = 100 €

In that scenario Depreciation starts in Mai 2024 and is calculated by 50 € between Mai and December 2024. From January 2025 onwards depreciation is 100 € until value gets 0 €:

I have already created one depreciation key and multilevel method for this special requirement, but as you see depreciation method should be treated differently depending on the FY.

My understanding is that the trigger of the method changeover needs to be the FY change.

In customizing I could not find anything that can support that behavior. So far the Depr. Key is calculating the 50% of the depreciation for the first 12 months (as customized in the multilevel method) and unfortunately I could not find any possibility to restrict the 50% depreciation rule to only the first FY.

Multilevel Meth.:

Depr. Key:

 

My research led me to the BAdI methods FAA_DC_CUSTOMER (define_changeover_yr) and FAA_DC_CUSTOMER (define_changeover_yr_and_prd). It seems that we can create new changeover rules for that.

 

Does anyone of you have experience with such/similar requirement and could explain me whether this BAdI implementation is the only possibility to comply with the requirement?

 

Thank you so much for your input.

 

Best regards

Thaís

entry.title: Re: Preliminary Currency Conversion in SAP Data Warehouse Cloud
entry.content:
Hi 

Struggling to get currency conversion to work in datasphere:

1. Using the currency_conversion function : 

getting this error: invalid name of function or procedure: CONVERT_CURRENCY: line 150 col 3 (at pos 13106)

Is this related to the schema name?

Where do you find the schema name for currency tables in SDS?

2. Currency converted column method get this error:

currency/unit conversion error: could not read currency precisions from table "00_ADMINISTRATION"."V_TCURX" - invalid column name: CURRKEY: line 1 col 8 (at pos 7) at SQLFrontend/SQLPreprocessor/Rules/Impl/NameResolution.cpp:1199

The V_TCURX - is created and loaded with data from S/4 system?

entry.title: Re: Issue with local annotations in sap bas
entry.content: @Anjan_3331 What is the OData version your project is based on?

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I assume that your question is about the advanced ICo sales process.

The sales order in the selling company is logistically directly integrated into the delivering plant. Therefore, the MRP run in the delivering plant considers the sales order in the selling company. Means the sales order in the selling company raises directly the demand in the delivering plant. 
The sales order in the delivering company is logistically not integrated with the delivering plant. The sales order in the delivering company reflects confirmed dates and quantities of the sales order in the selling company.
In advanced ICo sales process there is no ICo stock transport order, it's an ICo purchase order.

Advanced ICo sales is logistically a sell from stock scenario where the entered sales order (to the external customer) has direct access to the stock and can immediately reserve the stock.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content:
Hello experts, @Matthew_Shaw 

Need a help please?

I am trying to create a replication flow to replicate data from a local table in datasphere to AWS S3.  I want to replicate delta updates on hourly basis, not the full load. I can only see initial only option, I cannot see initial and delta. Can you please help me how to achieve delta replication as I need to run it every hour and don't want to load the systems with full load every time?

Thanks in advance. 

Regards

SK

entry.title: Re: Customizable/ Custom Fields 01-15
entry.content:
Within the People Profile, I've enabled the headers to show Customizable Field 1. See below.

There is already a date populated here. I cannot tell what that date relates to. Only a handful of users have this date populated.

Also, I'm looking to understand how these Customizable Fields are populated. For example, I would like to have something different in Customizable Field 1 like Gender or First Name & Middle Name & Last Name concatenated etc... I'm unable to see how to do this.

entry.title: SAP Business client 8.0 - Remove dotted line border during animated focus
entry.content:
Hello,

I just installed SAP GUI and SAP business client 8.0.
When opening the setting in usual SAP GUI / SAP logon, i have ticked animated focus.
If i open the setting from SAP business client, animated focus is unticked and I can not change it.
I have activate the setting to adopt SAP GUI setting from business client

Now, using SAP from Business client, on every transaction screen I have this focus on each field with dotted line border.

Any idea how to deactivate this border ? 

Thanks in advance

Vincent

entry.title: Re: Cumulativ Material Ledger initialization (MR22)
entry.content:
Hello Romain,

Thank you for your question.

After talking to the experts, I can provide the following answer: The feature mass debit/credit of materials is not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud as of now. 

You may request the feature on the Customer Influence Portal. We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback. 

Kind regards,

Jessica

maybe the issue is in the association to the VH? Try to add it to VH, or TEXT in the Element properties in the Custom CDS View app. 
Did you try AI to find a solution? Maybe it will help you better than I did 😄 

KR 
Veronika

entry.title: Re: How do we identify Create/Update/Delete actions in RA...
entry.content: Hi Abhishek, How do we identify whether the operation is CREATE or EDIT here?

entry.title: Improvement Request – Enhance to user experience of the Notification feed
entry.content:
May I please get the community's thoughts on the current notifications feed functionality/bugs and whether the request below would help improve the user experience? (Please ignore if this has already been discussed or planned for future releases.)

1) Upon logging into the SAP Community page, the notifications feed is my usual go-to place to catch up on the latest blogs and questions/discussions in my subscribed tags. In this case, I see 8 unread notifications.

After opening the notification feed, I saw that the unread notifications were highlighted with a blue banner. I was able to open only 3 out of the 8 unread notifications, intending to return later to the remaining ones.

The old notifications do not have a blue colour indicator, as they are considered read.

Based on the number of notifications in the feed, it is practically impossible to catch up with all unread notifications at once (here 8 notifications). It would be helpful if only opened notifications were marked as read, ensuring that unread ones remain highlighted for future reference and adding an option to mark all of them as read.

However, when I refresh or reload the page, all 8 notifications are marked as read, even though I did not open 5 of them, so it is difficult to continue where I left off.

2) Additionally, when there are a large number of notifications, the feed fails to paginate properly and gets stuck on the progress indicator, preventing access to older notifications. A smoother pagination experience would significantly enhance usability, especially for users catching up on the content after a few days.

 

 

3) Currently, there are no filtering options to sort notifications by category, such as Read/Unread, Blogs, or Q&A. Adding these filters would improve the user experience by allowing easier access to relevant notifications.

I hope these suggestions to the notifications feed will improve the user experience and I would love to hear the feedback from the community on these suggestions. 

Regards

Virinchy

entry.title: Re: Define Work Center Category Does Not Exist
entry.content: @Tim_Jones Could you please submit this comment as an answer instead, so that we can accept it as the correct solution to this question.

entry.title: Information needed for SAP Cloud ALM callback to close alerts linked to closed external tickets
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to perform the callback option to auto solve the alerts in SAP Cloud ALM.

Exactly where we have to configure that: under external api management tab ---> subscription management? or webhook management? or mapping management?

 

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Information needed for SAP Cloud ALM callback to clos...
entry.content:
The Integration with External Ticket systems guide contains section Configuration Flow. The sub-section "Setup Event Situation Confirmation if Ticket is Solved" outlines the following: "...the ticket system processing rules must be updated to trigger a callback into Cloud ALM using the Event Situation API."

The callback must be configured outside of SAP Cloud ALM. The SAP Cloud ALM External API Management app cannot be used to configure the callback. It is not possible to make SAP Cloud ALM perform the callback.

If your external ticketing system supports a processing rules functionality (or other similar functionality), the callback can be executed using that mechanism. The processing rule will need to:

  1. Read the details of the ticket being closed.
  2. Format the ticket data into a compliant JSON object.
  3. Call the SAP Cloud ALM Event Situation API and transmit the JSON object as the Request Body.

If your external ticketing software does not offer this feature, you will need to need to call the SAP Cloud ALM Event Situation API by some other mechanism. These could be:

  • API Middleware
  • REST API Client software
  • Custom software

More information about how to use the SAP Cloud ALM Event Situation API can be found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and the API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM pages for the API.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Exception handling
entry.content: How we can achieve for desktop WHT confirmation? BIDP and BIDF is build for RF environment.

entry.title: SAP UI5 Routing and Navigation - Detailed
entry.content:
In SAP UI5 routing, different types of brackets are used to define patterns and parameters in the routing configuration. Here are the main types and their uses:

  1. Curly Braces {}:

    • Usage: Used to define mandatory parameters in the route pattern.
    • Example: In the pattern product/{productId}, {productId} is a mandatory parameter that must be provided in the URL.
    • Purpose: Ensures that the route is only matched if the parameter is present in the URL.

  2. Colon ::

    • Usage: Used to define optional parameters in the route pattern.
    • Example: In the pattern product/:productId:, :productId: is an optional parameter that may or may not be provided in the URL.
    • Purpose: Allows the route to be matched even if the parameter is not present in the URL.

  3. Square Brackets []:

    • Usage: Used to define optional parts of the route pattern.
    • Example: In the pattern product[/:productId], /:productId is an optional part of the pattern.
    • Purpose: Allows flexibility in the URL structure, making parts of the pattern optional.

Examples

  1. Mandatory Parameter:

    {
    "pattern": "product/{productId}",
    "name": "productDetail",
    "target": "productDetail"
}
    • URL Example: #/product/123 (123 is the productId)

  2. Optional Parameter:

    {
    "pattern": "product/:productId:",
    "name": "productDetail",
    "target": "productDetail"
}
    • URL Example: #/product/123 or #/product/ (123 is the optional productId)

  3. Optional Part of the Pattern:

    {
    "pattern": "product[/:productId]",
    "name": "productDetail",
    "target": "productDetail"
}
    • URL Example: #/product/123 or #/product (123 is the optional productId)

These brackets help define the structure and flexibility of the routes in your SAP UI5 application, making it easier to handle different URL patterns and parameters.

In SAP UI5 routing, you can define routes and targets in various ways to handle different navigation scenarios. Here are some common types and configurations:

Routes

  1. Basic Route:

    • Example:
      {
    "name": "View1",
    "pattern": "",
    "target": ["TargetView1"]
}
    • Description: A simple route with no parameters, navigating to View1.

  2. Parameterized Route:

    • Example:
      {
    "name": "ProductDetail",
    "pattern": "product/{productId}",
    "target": ["TargetProductDetail"]
}
    • Description: A route with a mandatory parameter productId.

  3. Optional Parameter Route:

    • Example:
      {
    "name": "ProductDetail",
    "pattern": "product/:productId:",
    "target": ["TargetProductDetail"]
}
    • Description: A route with an optional parameter productId.

  4. Multiple Parameters Route:

    • Example:
      {
    "name": "OrderDetail",
    "pattern": "order/{orderId}/item/{itemId}",
    "target": ["TargetOrderDetail"]
}
    • Description: A route with multiple parameters orderId and itemId.

  5. Wildcard Route:

    • Example:
      {
    "name": "NotFound",
    "pattern": "*",
    "target": ["TargetNotFound"]
}
    • Description: A wildcard route that matches any URL not defined by other routes.

Targets

  1. Basic Target:

    • Example:
      {
    "TargetView1": {
        "viewName": "View1",
        "viewLevel": 1
    }
}
    • Description: A simple target pointing to View1.

  2. Target with Control:

    • Example:
      {
    "TargetView1": {
        "viewName": "View1",
        "controlId": "app",
        "controlAggregation": "pages"
    }
}
    • Description: A target specifying the control and aggregation where the view should be placed.

  3. Multiple Views Target:

    • Example:
      {
    "TargetMasterDetail": {
        "viewName": ["MasterView", "DetailView"],
        "controlId": "splitApp",
        "controlAggregation": ["masterPages", "detailPages"]
    }
}
    • Description: A target that includes multiple views, such as in a master-detail scenario.

  4. Target with Transition:

    • Example:
      {
    "TargetView1": {
        "viewName": "View1",
        "transition": "slide"
    }
}
    • Description: A target specifying a transition effect.

Example Configuration

Combining these elements, your manifest.json might look like this:

{
    "routing": {
        "config": {
            "routerClass": "sap.m.routing.Router",
            "controlAggregation": "pages",
            "controlId": "app",
            "transition": "slide",
            "viewType": "XML",
            "viewPath": "routingandnavigation.view",
            "async": true
        },
        "routes": [
            {
                "name": "View1",
                "pattern": "",
                "target": ["TargetView1"]
            },
            {
                "name": "View2",
                "pattern": "View2",
                "target": ["TargetView2"]
            },
            {
                "name": "ProductDetail",
                "pattern": "product/{productId}",
                "target": ["TargetProductDetail"]
            }
        ],
        "targets": {
            "TargetView1": {
                "viewName": "View1",
                "controlId": "app",
                "controlAggregation": "pages"
            },
            "TargetView2": {
                "viewName": "View2",
                "controlId": "app",
                "controlAggregation": "pages"
            },
            "TargetProductDetail": {
                "viewName": "ProductDetail",
                "controlId": "app",
                "controlAggregation": "pages"
            }
        }
    }
}

This configuration demonstrates various types of routes and targets, providing flexibility in how you navigate within your SAP UI5 application.

entry.title: Re: Define Work Center Category Does Not Exist
entry.content: That configuration isn't available in Public Cloud. Why dont you just create a Work Center and in the 'General Data' section choose 'Work Center Category = 0005 Plant Maintenance' and 'Usage = 004 Only Maintenance Task Lists'

entry.title: Re: Improvement Request – Enhance to user experience of the Notification feed
entry.content:
Hello @Virinchy 

That is a very good proposal indeed. Additionally I'd suggest the have an option to mark all or selected notifications as read. 

Also it'd good to receive only one notification per new post even if the post is assigned to multiple tags. Currently if a post is marked with multiple tags that I follow, I get as many notification as there are the tags. That's annoying.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Is the cross selling functionality available in the p...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, cross-selling functionality is available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. It leverages integrated sales and distribution processes to help businesses identify and promote additional products that customers might purchase, thereby enhancing sales opportunities. You can find more about specific features and how to implement cross-selling in the SAP Help Portal under sales and distribution modules. If you're looking for detailed guidance or extensibility options, checking the "Key User Extensibility" and "Developer Extensibility" sections might provide further insights.

References:
1 Business Partner Relationship - Receive Confirmation from Client to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
2 APIs for Data Exchange within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Systems
3 Business Partner - Receive Confirmation from Client to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

4 Organizational Links Between Sales and Other Areas

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Become a Paid Expert for Ask an Expert Peer program! NEW BTP Products available!
entry.content:
Hi @Virinchy ,

Fiori Launchpad should be available, I reached out to the vendor, I will circle back once it is added back.

Thanks for drawing our attention to it.

Best
Ervin

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori client on APP Store
entry.content:
Please note that the Fiori Client was removed from the Apple App Store and Google Play Store on March 31st, 2022.

Fiori Client is not a recommended option anymore, if required you may try to build it locally (it would not be a straightforward approach). Refer to the below links for more details. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIORI_CLIENT

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-plans-on-removing-the-fiori-client-from-mobile-app-stores-what-now/qaq-p/12364103

Regards

Virinchy

entry.title: Re: I cannot post a VL01N material document because a com...
entry.content:
Hello @CarlosG1254 

You need to adjust the delivery quantity to the picked quantity. You can do that in VL02N with the menu option Edit -> Copy Picked Quantity as Delivery Quantity for WM integrated deliveries. A delivery needs to be fully picked before goods issue posting.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Manual Bank Reconciliation using manage bank statements
entry.content:
Hi Nithin Pranesh, 

@nithin_pranesh 

You can easily create processing rules in the Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements app. For more information, see How to Create Processing Rules and Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements. If you'd like to set up processing rule with two posting steps, you need to automate the processing rule first. See also How to Automate Processing Rules. Furthermore, the following What's New document from the "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" 2502 release (February release) provides you some hints on how to set a reconciliation account for AP/AR postings in your processing rules in the Manage Processing Rules app: Reconciliation Account in Processing Rules (this last link is only available for a few months, though). 

I hope the linked help pages are useful for you to set up your posting and processing process. 

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
Hi @CarlosBecar and @ringofchaos ,

thanks for the feedback and bringing this up. Once we have more news, this blog post will be updated.

Hi @Chad_He ,

thanks for asking. Our delivery targets the products listed in the post above. You can see that the ABAP AI SDK is already available for OnPrem. In case there are further news, this blog post will be updated.

entry.title: SAP Intelligence - Delete processes, but that the pipelines remain.
entry.content:
Dear all, 

I would like to reduce the number of our cases in SAP Intelligence. However, I would like to delete the processes, but in such a way that the pipelines continue to exist.
To do this, I wanted to select the processes to be deleted and delete the process under Settings. I seem to have two options here. Which option would be correct to reduce the number of cases but keep the pipelines? Is there a possibility?

Thanks in advance. 
Best regards, 
Christine

entry.title: Re: SAP FSM+SAP S/4 Public cloud - integration equipment...
entry.content:
Hello,

The value of counter or other measurements is not supported by the standard integration between SAP Field Service Management and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

You need add custom fields and custom integration function to make it happen. Both SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP FSM provide APIs that can be utilized to sync data, including custom fields. You may need to implement custom integration logic to bridge the data between the two systems.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: to be withdraw
entry.content: To be deleted

entry.title: 通过限时促销免费获取SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502中的AI辅助功能！
entry.content:
在SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition的2502版本中，SAP推出了一系列创新的AI辅助功能。为了让更多企业能够体验和应用这些前沿技术，以智能高效的方式，提升日常工作效率，优化业务协作体验。

目前，2502版本中包含的四个实用的AI辅助功能将通过 limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition，免费提供给申请用户限时使用。

下面让我们简单了解下这四个功能：

AI辅助智能汇总(AI-Assisted Smart Summarization)：在使用SAP Fiori元素的应用程序里，它能对业务对象页面进行汇总。这些汇总结果实用性极高，比如您可以快速将其复制粘贴到邮件中，开启后续操作，十分便捷。

AI辅助轻松过滤器(AI-Assisted Easy Filter）：在基于SAP Fiori元素开发的清单报表应用程序中，以往繁琐的筛选操作成为过去式。现在，您只需通过自然语言输入筛选条件，就能获取精准的查询结果，实现最贴合自身需求的过滤体验，极大提高了数据筛选的效率。

AI辅助财务业务洞察（AI-Assisted Financial Business Insights: Review Booklet for Cost Centers)：成本中心审查手册里的数据常常复杂难懂，但有了这个功能，一切迎刃而解。它能帮您挖掘数据背后的意义，突出数据间的依赖关系，还能用自然语言快速解释和汇总洞察，并且给出相应的操作建议，助力企业更高效地进行财务决策。

AI辅助固定资产折旧码注释（AI-assisted Explanation of Fixed Asset Depreciation Keys）：固定资产折旧码设置复杂，分析不同折旧区域的资产价值难度不小。而强大的SAP Business AI可以为您详细解释折旧码的运行方式以及折旧计算系统背后的原理，让您轻松理解折旧码的配置设置，有效提升资产会计和财务报告的效率与决策质量。

此次免费的限时促销的申请时间截止到2025年7月31日，中国地区目前仅支持台湾和香港的客户申请。用户可以通过limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition的链接提交申请，尽早体验这些创新功能。

让我们一起拥抱AI时代，开启企业高效运营的新篇章！

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud reference is present in S/4HA...
entry.content:
Hi,

You're raising a good point and after some digging I could find some SAP notes giving a bit more insight on the topic:

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3016862: "note analyzer" note has been updated and it seems there's now a dedicated program to check latest DBC notes: CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_BDC.
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3500131: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3499606: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud - Security Settings

These notes lead me to this blog: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing which explains the new data integration concept.

Hope this helps!

Regards,

Clément

entry.title: Re: Product Allocation tables
entry.content: It is stored in PASQNC

entry.title: Error loading the sales orders file using the Import Sales Orders tile
entry.content:
when I load the attached file with the Import sales orders tile, SAP displays the following error message:
Your file contains missing, invalid, or duplicate data. Number of messages: 2.

Error :

Sales order 141: Item ID 10 exists more than once.
Sheet "Order Data", rows 34 and 254
----> Sales order 141: Item ID 10 exists more than once.
Row 254 not uploaded

Sales order 151: Item ID 10 exists more than once.
Sheet "Order Data", rows 48 and 264
---->Sales order 151: Item ID 10 exists more than once.
Row 264 not uploaded

 

When I separate the lines linked to the Sales Order (Temporary ID): 141 & 151 in another file--> the two sales orders are created correctly.

I have compared lines 34 and 254 as well as 48 and 264 and I have not identified any relationship or data duplication.
Could you please tell us why this is happening? Our users do not understand this error and do not wish to separate the lines into other files as this requires them to spend additional time analysing and working, bearing in mind that the files are supplied by another department.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Become a Paid Expert for Ask an Expert Peer program! NEW BTP Products available!
entry.content:
Thanks @ErvinSzolke . 

In the meantime, I registered and was selected for other areas within my expertise. I also received an alert for biweekly expert discussions, with the next session happening tonight at 3:30 AM Sydney time.

Are there any alternative time slots available, or is there a way I can coordinate internally for a discussion? I truly don’t want to miss these calls.

Regards

Virinchy

entry.title: Re: Creating a Multi-Item Edit Screen in RAP Similar to SM30
entry.content:
• Feature Control: Make the business key editable only during creation.

@MioYasutake How can we achieve this?

entry.title: initiallyVisibleFields SmartTable property not working with JSONModel-based SmartTable
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a SmartTable that takes data from a simple JSON model instead of an oData service. This is possible as you can see from here.
The table has many columns (I won't list them all for convenience), so I would like to display only some of them in the beginning. I tried to use the initiallyVisibleFields property by inserting the name of the data as it is written in the JSON model (and also as I recall it in the items), but when I go to test all the columns are still visible.

All data is shown correctly in the rows.

What am I doing wrong?

 

 

<st:SmartTable
    entitySet="BPSet"
    tableBindingPath="BPModel>/"
    header="{i18n>contractsList}"
    showRowCount="true"
    tableType="Table"
    showFullScreenButton="false"
    useVariantManagement="false"
    enableAutoBinding="true"
    enableExport="true"
    useTablePersonalisation="true"
    initiallyVisibleFields="Ebeln,Name1"
>
    <st:customToolbar>
        <m:OverflowToolbar design="Transparent">
            <m:ToolbarSpacer />
            <m:Button
                id="ButtonFilter"
                type="Emphasized"
                icon="sap-icon://filter"
                text="{i18n>contractsAllowed}"
                width="auto"
                enabled="true"
                iconDensityAware="false"
                press="contractsAllowedFilter"
            />
            <m:Button
                id="ButtonRefresh"
                type="Emphasized"
                text="{i18n>refresh}"
                tooltip="{i18n>refresh}"
                press="getRunItems"
                icon="sap-icon://refresh"
            />
            <m:Button
                id="ButtonTacitRenewal"
                type="Emphasized"
                text="{i18n>tacitRenewal}"
                tooltip="{i18n>tacitRenewal}"
                width="auto"
                enabled="true"
                iconDensityAware="false"
                press="onTacitRenewal"
            />
            <m:ToolbarSpacer />
        </m:OverflowToolbar>
    </st:customToolbar>
    <m:Table
        id="detailContractsTable"
        mode="MultiSelect"
        itemPress="toListaDoc"
    >
        <m:columns>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "Ebeln", "leadingProperty": "Ebeln"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>ContractId}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "Name1", "leadingProperty": "Name1"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>Name}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "createdAt", "leadingProperty": "createdAt"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>creationDate}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "createdBy", "leadingProperty": "createdBy"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>createdBy}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "modifiedAt", "leadingProperty": "modifiedAt"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>modifiedAt}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "modifiedBy", "leadingProperty": "modifiedBy"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>modifiedBy}" />
            </m:Column>
            <m:Column>
                <m:customData>
                    <core:CustomData
                        key="p13nData"
                        value='{"columnKey": "Status", "leadingProperty": "Status"}'
                    />
                </m:customData>
                <m:Text text="{i18n>status}" />
            </m:Column>
        </m:columns>
        <m:items>
            <m:ColumnListItem
                type="Navigation"
                press="onPress"
            >
                <m:cells>
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>Ebeln}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>Name1}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>createdAt}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>createdBy}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>modifiedAt}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>modifiedBy}" />
                    <m:Text text="{BPModel>Status}" />
                </m:cells>
            </m:ColumnListItem>
        </m:items>
    </m:Table>
</st:SmartTable>

entry.title: Dependency in POD
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I would like to know how to set up the dependency for each activity in the POD. For example, with the Default Work Center POD, I was able to collect data without starting the SFC. Now, I want to restrict data collection if the SFC status is "Not Started".

entry.title: Re: Become a Paid Expert for Ask an Expert Peer program! NEW BTP Products available!
entry.content:
Hi @Virinchy ,

I am afraid I cannot answer this as that event is organised by Movate, not by SAP.

If you have questions I recommend to reach out to Movate using their chat application (write in to the "Ask a Peer" widget (on the Movate website)).

I hope this helps.

Best,
Ervin

entry.title: Re: Is the cross selling functionality available in the p...
entry.content:
Hi Dequan,

Thank you for your answer. Is there any specific link that you can provide? I cannot find any documentation about implementing cross selling functionality in public cloud.

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution in SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
@Abhishek_Ramesh Abhishek

 

Dear Abhishek,
It would be great to hear your comments on the following questions.
when will it be possible to use common divisions for service with adv.execution?
and when it will be possible to use phase modell EAM orders with advanced execution?

Thanks and Kind Regards,
Michaela

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Inbuilt Retry option for HTTP Receiver Adapter
entry.content: @morris91 same here any update yet?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution in SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
@michi_500 

Hello Michaela,

Regarding support of phase model in billable maintenance orders: this would be supported with one of the feature packs in SAP S/4HANA 2025, but it is definitely on the roadmap.

Regarding common divisions, please could you elaborate what this means? I am not able to follow this concept of "common divisions".

Thanks and Kind Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Sap analytics cloud Excel add in routes to white scre...
entry.content:
@Eliram_Lugassi 

May be refer to the below SAP notes and see if they apply to your case.

3083876 - Blank logon screen when trying to logon to SAC from SAC, Add-in for MS Office - SAP for Me

3085993 - Troubleshooting guide for the SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Office - SAP for Me

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: How to export vendors invoices by material code from SAP
entry.content: in ECC, how can I export vendor invoices, or Open & ALL items with the material code?

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content: Thanks @Mark_P_Burke for the great care and detailed explanation in all the raised topics. It will be of great benefit to me and others in the SAP community the insights and scenarios given, and I'll give it a try and use this approach going forward. I did use some of the Rapid FP&A for inspiration in dashboards and reports, great work by the way 🙂

entry.title: Re: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content:
hi @amarseli001 

The way I see this, that document is an older version to what Note 3255746 says (Last version updated late 2024);

"The Operational Data Provisioning (ODP) framework for data extraction and replication is exposed by an official and externally-available OData API and can still be used (link to documentation)"

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/ccc9cdbdc6cd4eceaf1e5485b1bf8f4b/11853413cf124dde91925284133c007d.html?locale=en-US

That said, I must find some of this information on S/4HANA

entry.title: Get PDF Action in Sales Contract API | S4 Output | PDF to external system
entry.content:
Is it possible to enhance the Sales Contract API(SAP Business Accelerator Hub) to include a Get PDF action similar to what is avaialable in the Billing Document API?

Get PDF of Billing Document | SAP Help Portal

We are trying to send the form output generated from the Sales contract to an external system. We are able to easily do it for billing documents since the API has the standard operation for PDF but the same is not available for Sales Contracts.For output management,we are using S4 output.

entry.title: Re: Delegation for Performance Reviews 2H 2024 Release Fe...
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This might not exactly answer your question, but I will share how we have set up PDD delegation permissions, after we experienced some challenges too:

RBP: Performance Reviewer (Delegation):

Assignments:

 

 

We also have a second parallel RBP following discussion with Support:

RBP: Delegation for Performance Reviews:

Assignments:

For the 2nd RBP: in our Granted Group - the 'PDD Delegators' are defined by grade - this covers all salaried colleagues who may have direct reports with PDD Forms.

The Target Group - the 'PDD Delegatees' are defined by grade too - this covers a slightly smaller group of salaried colleagues who may need to accept responsibility for delegated PDD Forms, i.e. this group contains the users who will gain access to the delegated PDD Forms.

We are still experiencing challenges with random and inconsistent error messages when using the tool, but in general, the above config has worked. 

Regards,

Charlotte

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution in SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
@Abhishek_Ramesh 
Dear Abhisek

Thanks for your quick response.
In sales we use common division for the Business partners.
So we need only to create the BP for one sales org and one division. In the sales order we can choose for which division the order should be created.
We only need to create the customer once and then specify the division when setting up the sales-order
A commonly used scenario in sales .and reduces the effort required when creating customers.
But Service is not able to work with it. Service module expects division 00 (common division) where the customer is created, if we change it to division 01 (spare parts) the system generates the error message..customer not created for division 01.
I hope i explained it well
Best regards,
Michaela

entry.title: Re: Restrict Delete Facts in Modeler but allow user to pl...
entry.content:
Hi @mark_glowczewski2 ,  I am also the same issue. Was this solved. If yes, can you please let me know what needs to be done. I have the role to prevent deletion, but it is not working.

Thanks,

Bhakti

entry.title: Re: Image is going out of window
entry.content: change resolution in window greater will small the image and also you can align as you prefer. some images have resolution problem

entry.title: Re: Lowering the SFC quantity In SAP DM
entry.content: This has worked even though set SFC quantity isn't support for relaxed workflows for raising the Quantity, what other api's do you recommend as I'm facing an issue reducing the SFC quantity

entry.title: Re: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - The Collection Blog
entry.content: HI @ebsor - check out the community page for master data governance here - Master Data | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Community
